briefs.title: Burnaby Council Supports Liquor License for Solo Karaoke on Kingsway
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to support a liquor primary license for Solo Karaoke, located at 102-6462 Kingsway. This decision follows a review of previous allegations. Council will recommend to the Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch (LCRB) that the license be issued.
briefs.action_required: Ensure your business operations align with all conditions outlined by the LCRB for the liquor primary license.
briefs.source_body: 7.1 LLA #25-12 - 102-6462 KINGSWAY (SOLO KARAOKE) - UPDATE The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report summarizing the findings of the investigation related to previous allegations, and to provide Council with a recommendation to support the subject liquor primary licence. MOVED AND SECONDED Resolution No. 2026 - 121 THAT a recommendation be provided to the Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch that a liquor primary licence be issued to Solo Karaoke at 102–6462 Open Council Meeting Minutes March 10, 2026 5 Kingsway, as outlined in the report titled “LLA #25-12 - 102–6462 Kingsway (Solo Karaoke) - Update” dated March 10, 2026. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY Minutes

briefs.title: Burnaby forwards Metro 2050 amendment letter, adding food security and affordability.
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted to send a comment letter to the Metro Vancouver Board regarding proposed changes to Metro 2050, a regional growth strategy. The letter includes concerns about food affordability and security, in addition to other topics.
briefs.industries: Agriculture, Grocery & Food Retail, Food Service
briefs.source_body:
7.2 
COMMENTS ON PROPOSED METRO 2050 AMENDMENT OPTIONS IN 
RESPONSE TO REQUEST BY THE MAYORS OF SURREY, LANGLEY 
TOWNSHIP AND DELTA 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking 
Council approval to forward the attached comment letter to the Metro Vancouver 
Board conveying concerns about the four proposed Metro 2050 amendment 
options. 
MOVED AND SECONDED  
THAT the letter provided as Attachment 2 to the report titled “Comments on 
Proposed Metro 2050 Amendment Options in Response to Request by Mayors of 
Surrey, Langley Township and Delta”, dated March 10, 2026, be forwarded to the 
Metro Vancouver Board. 
AMENDED 
Arising from discussion, Councillor Gu introduced the following amendment: 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
THAT the letter provided as Attachment 2, be AMENDED, to add the topics of 
food affordability and food security and their impact. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY 
 
 
The amended motion was then put to a final vote: 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 122 
THAT the letter provided as Attachment 2, AS AMENDED, to the report titled 
“Comments on Proposed Metro 2050 Amendment Options in Response to 
Request by Mayors of Surrey, Langley Township and Delta”, dated March 10, 
2026, be forwarded to the Metro Vancouver Board. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approved BC Hydro Right-of-Way at 4111 Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved granting BC Hydro a Statutory Right-of-Way. This gives BC Hydro the right to install, operate, and maintain equipment on a part of the property at 4111 Norland Avenue. This is needed for the Community Safety Building project. A 'Statutory Right-of-Way' is a legal agreement that allows a company to use someone else's land for a specific purpose.
briefs.source_body:
7.3 
STATUTORY RIGHT OF WAY FOR BC HYDRO - 4111 NORLAND AVENUE 
(COMMUNITY SAFETY BUILDING) 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking 
Council approval to grant a Statutory Right-of-Way over a portion of 4111 Norland 
Avenue to permit the installation, operations and maintenance of works. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 123 
THAT the granting and execution of any required Statutory Rights-of-Way over a 
portion of 4111 Norland Avenue, in favour of third‑party utility providers for the 
installation of infrastructure necessary to service the Community Safety Building 
project, be approved. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby to apply for UBCM grant for development approvals program.
briefs.summary: Burnaby will apply for a grant of up to $200,000 from the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM). The money would fund the city's development approvals program. This program helps manage development applications.
briefs.source_body:
7.4 
GA - APPLICATION TO UBCM LOCAL GOVERNMENT DEVELOPMENT 
APPROVALS PROGRAM GRANT FUNDING (2026) 
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking 
Council endorsement to apply for the 2026 UBCM Local Government 
Development Approvals funding program. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 124 
THAT staff be authorized to submit a grant application to the UBCM Local 
Government Development Approvals Program, for up to $200,000 in grant 
funding; 
THAT the proposed program activities, as described in Section 3.0 of the report 
titled “GA – Application to UBCM Local Government Development Approvals 
Program Grant Funding (2026)” dated March 10, 2026, be supported; and, 
THAT staff undertake overall grant management, as required by the UBCM Local 
Government Development Approvals program. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves ServiceNow Contract for IT Ticketing Software Licenses
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a contract with ServiceNow, Inc. for IT ticketing software licenses. The contract is for an estimated $580,985, including taxes, and covers the period from March 16, 2026, to March 15, 2029. Final payment will depend on the actual goods and services delivered.
briefs.source_body: 7.5 CA - IT TICKETING SOFTWARE LICENSES The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for IT Ticketing Software Licenses. MOVED AND SECONDED Open Council Meeting Minutes March 10, 2026 7 Resolution No. 2026 - 125 THAT a contract award to ServiceNow, Inc. for an estimated cost of $580,985 including taxes, from March 16, 2026 to March 15, 2029, as outlined in the report titled “CA – IT Ticketing Software Licenses” dated March 10, 2026, be approved; and THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services delivered and hourly rates submitted. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY Minutes

briefs.title: Burnaby Adopts Amendments to Community Grant Policy and Application Process.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved changes to the Community Grant Policy and the Community Grant Application. These changes aim to improve the grant process, but the details of the amendments were not specified in the report.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Community Grant Policy and Application for any upcoming grant opportunities.
briefs.source_body:
8.1 
ECC - COMMUNITY GRANT POLICY AND COMMUNITY GRANT 
APPLICATION AMENDMENTS 
The Executive Committee of Council (ECC) submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for amendments to the Community Grant Policy and Community Grant 
Application. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 127 
THAT the amendments to the Community Grant Policy and Community Grant 
Application, as indicated in Attachment 1 and Attachment 2 of the report titled 
"Community Grant Policy and Community Grant Application Amendments", dated 
March 3, 2026, of the Executive Committee of Council meeting, be adopted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Parks, Recreation, and Culture Plan; Public Engagement OK'd
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the draft Parks, Recreation, and Culture Plan. Council also authorized staff to proceed with the second round of public engagement, as outlined in Section 4.0 of the report.
briefs.source_body: 8.2 PRCC - DRAFT PRC PLAN The Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee (PRCC) submitted a report providing information on the draft Making Life Better: Parks, Recreation and Culture (PRC) Plan. MOVED AND SECONDED Resolution No. 2026 - 126 THAT the draft Making Life Better: Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan, as provided in Attachment 1 of the report titled "Draft PRC Plan", dated February 25, 2026, of the Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee, be approved; and Open Council Meeting Minutes March 10, 2026 8 THAT staff be authorized to proceed with the second round of public engagement, as outlined in Section 4.0 of this report. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY Minutes

briefs.title: Burnaby supports Tourism Burnaby's bid for 2028 Urban Forest Conference.
briefs.summary: Council voted to support Tourism Burnaby's effort to host the Canadian Urban Forest Conference in 2028. This shows the City's backing as Tourism Burnaby prepares its bid to host the conference.
briefs.source_body:
8.3 
PRCC - TOURISM BURNABY: REQUEST FOR SUPPORT FOR 2028 
CANADIAN URBAN FOREST CONFERENCE 
The Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee (PRCC) submitted a report 
seeking Council approval, in principle, for the Tourism Burnaby's pursuit of a bid 
to host the 2028 Canadian Urban Forest Conference. 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 128 
THAT Tourism Burnaby's pursuit of a bid to host the 2028 Canadian Urban 
Forest Conference, as discussed at the February 25, 2026, Parks, Recreation 
and Culture Committee meeting, be supported in principle. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby to review off-street parking payment plan; new parking space feasibility study.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council asked staff to review a plan for new off-street parking. The plan involves using money from the Off-Street Parking Reserve Fund. It also includes rezoning and a study to see if new parking spaces are possible on Pender Street. Staff must report back in four months.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body: 8.4 PDC - PROPOSED OFF-STREET PARKING SPACES - PAYMENT IN-LIEU The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking Council approval to access the Off-Street Parking Reserve Fund and initiate rezoning and a feasibility study to support the development of new off-street parking spaces at 4301/4305 and 4171 Pender Street. MOVED AND SECONDED THAT the report titled "Proposed Off-Street Parking Spaces - Payment In-Lieu" dated March 4, 2026, of the Planning and Development Committee meeting, be REFERRED to staff for further review. AMENDED Arising from discussion, Councillor Tetrault introduced the following amendment: MOVED AND SECONDED THAT the report titled "Proposed Off-Street Parking Spaces - Payment In-Lieu" dated March 4, 2026, of the Planning and Development Committee meeting, be REFERRED to staff for further review and report back to Council within four months. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY Open Council Meeting Minutes March 10, 2026 9 The amended motion was then put to a final vote: MOVED AND SECONDED Resolution No. 2026 - 129 THAT the report titled "Proposed Off-Street Parking Spaces - Payment In-Lieu" dated March 4, 2026, of the Planning and Development Committee meeting, be REFERRED to staff for further review and report back to Council within four months. CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY Minutes

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Parking and EV Charging Bylaw: What Businesses Need to Know
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to amend Bylaw 14805, which covers parking meters and electric vehicle (EV) charging meters. The changes are made through Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026. This update affects how parking and EV charging are managed in the city.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw to understand changes to parking and EV charging regulations.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1 #14805 - Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter 
Bylaw 2019, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 137-1 
THAT Bylaw No. 14805 be now read a first, second and third time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby amended fees and charges bylaw.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to amend Bylaw 14806, the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This bylaw sets out the fees and charges for various services and permits provided by the City of Burnaby. Changes to fees could affect your business costs.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated fees and charges in Bylaw 14806 to assess the impact on your business.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2 #14806 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment 
Bylaw No. 2, 2026 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 137-2 
THAT Bylaw No. 14806 be now read a first, second and third time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Amended Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to amend the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This bylaw helps the city enforce its bylaws by allowing it to issue tickets for violations. The changes aim to improve the process of issuing and handling these tickets.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.3 #14807 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment 
Bylaw No. 2, 2026 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 137-3 
THAT Bylaw No. 14807 be now read a first, second and third time. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby adopted Zoning Bylaw 14792, amending zoning rules within the city.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved Bylaw 14792. The bylaw amends the city's zoning rules. Zoning rules control how land can be used. The changes are made through an amendment to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1 #14792 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026 - 
Text Amendment 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 138 
THAT Bylaw No. 14792 be now read a third time and final time and adopted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approved Zoning Bylaw Amendment for 7135 Walker Ave. and 7244 Arcola St.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council adopted Amendment Bylaw 14744. This bylaw changes the zoning for the properties at 7135 Walker Ave. and 7244 Arcola St. A zoning bylaw controls how land can be used. This change is now in effect.
briefs.source_body:
11.3.1 #14744 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2025 - 
REZ #24-20 (7135 Walker Ave. and 7244 Arcola St.) 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 139-1 
THAT Bylaw No. 14744 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approved Zoning Bylaw Amendment REZ #25-05 for Still Creek Ave
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council adopted Bylaw 14763, amending the zoning bylaw for 3944 and 3942 Still Creek Avenue. This zoning change is now in effect. A zoning bylaw controls how land can be used and developed.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated zoning details for 3944 and 3942 Still Creek Avenue to understand the changes.

11.3.2 #14763 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 15, 2025 -

THAT Bylaw No. 14763 be now read a final time and adopted.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Adopts Zoning Bylaw Amendment for 5889 Barker Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved an amendment to the zoning bylaw. Bylaw 14782 changes the zoning for the property at 5889 Barker Avenue. This decision is now final and adopted by the council.

11.3.3 #14782 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 19, 2025 -

briefs.title: Burnaby Amended Business Licence Bylaw No. 14798 for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to adopt Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026, which amends the Business Licence Bylaw 2017. The changes are now in effect. A business licence is a permit to operate a business in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Business Licence Bylaw to ensure your Burnaby business complies.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Spa & Wellness, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Grocery & Food Retail, Healthcare, Finance & Insurance, Technology, Natural Resources, Transportation & Logistics, Agriculture, Professional Services, Liquor, Cannabis, Construction & Development, Labour & Employment

11.3.4 #14798 - Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017, Amendment Bylaw No. 
1, 2026 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 139-4 
THAT Bylaw No. 14798 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby amended its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw.
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to approve changes to the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This bylaw helps the city enforce its rules by allowing it to issue tickets for violations. The changes aim to improve how the city handles bylaw enforcement.

briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to adopt Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026, which changes the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw 14800. This bylaw outlines the various fees and charges for different services and permits provided by the City of Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated fees and charges in Bylaw 14800 to understand any changes affecting your business.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Adopts Amendment to Procedure Bylaw 14801 for 2024
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved an amendment to the Procedure Bylaw 14801 for 2024. This bylaw outlines the rules for how council meetings are run. The amendment is officially called "Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026."
briefs.source_body:
11.3.7 #14801 - Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 139-7 
THAT Bylaw No. 14801 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby to Apply Tenant Eligibility Criteria for New Rezoning Applications
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council decided to use specific criteria to determine which tenants are eligible for protection during new rezoning applications. This applies to development sites that already have a rezoning application in progress. The goal is to maintain the original eligibility date for tenant protections.
briefs.action_required: Landlords should familiarize themselves with the tenant eligibility criteria for rezoning applications.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body:
14.1 Resolution No. C2026 - 023 
THAT staff be directed to apply the criteria, as outlined in Attachment 1 of this 
report (Option 1: Maintain Original Eligibility Date), to determine tenant eligibility 
for new rezoning applications, in respect of development sites with an in-stream 
rezoning application; and 
THAT the recommendation and Attachment 1 of the report be released to the 
Open Council meeting on March 10, 2026. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
